themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, where President Trump was present, became a dominant transnational story. Right-wing outlets across the U.S., Italy, Spain, and beyond framed the event as emblematic of rising political violence, security failures, or a broader crisis of governance. The incident’s rapid spread reflects its perceived symbolic weight in right-wing narratives about threats to conservative leaders.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense coverage of Trump’s evacuation, suspect’s motives, and Secret Service response, with emphasis on the dangers of the presidency and political polarization.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Focus on Meloni’s reaction to the shooting, framing it as part of a broader pattern of political violence and instability in Western democracies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Coverage linked the shooting to broader critiques of U.S. political dysfunction, with some outlets suggesting it reflects the dangers of left-wing extremism.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Highlighted the incident as evidence of growing threats to political figures, tying it to broader concerns about security and free speech.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Used the event to criticize U.S. political instability and draw parallels to perceived threats against far-right leaders in Europe.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: The AfD in Germany and Vox in Spain continue to dominate polling, reflecting a broader trend of far-right gains across Europe. This theme, ongoing from the previous run, highlights the normalization of far-right rhetoric and policy demands, particularly around immigration and national sovereignty. The convergence remains strong as these parties shape coalition dynamics and policy debates in multiple countries.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD reaches record 28% in polls, leading CDU/CSU and complicating coalition options. Coverage emphasizes its growing influence and internal controversies, such as expulsions for extremist rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s polling strength and policy influence force PP into regional coalitions, with its 'national priority' agenda dominating Spanish political discourse.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella strategize for 2027 elections, focusing on immigration and economic policies while facing financial challenges.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD’s influence in Swedish politics grows, with debates over its role in government and impact on migration policies.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP criticizes Norway’s energy policies, framing them as politically motivated and harmful to rural communities.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of PP-Vox coalitions in Spain’s Extremadura and Aragón regions has ignited a cross-national debate about the legality and morality of far-right policies, particularly the 'national priority' clause. This theme, ongoing from the previous run, highlights how regional pacts are reshaping national political discourse and testing constitutional limits on immigration and social welfare policies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s 'national priority' policy, which restricts public aid to Spanish citizens, dominates regional coalitions with PP, sparking legal challenges and public backlash.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP’s alliances with Vox in Extremadura and Aragón force the party to adopt far-right policies, creating internal divisions and public controversy.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 PSOE
countries.note: PSOE condemns PP-Vox pacts as unconstitutional and discriminatory, launching campaigns to counter their influence in upcoming elections.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán’s decision to step down from Hungary’s parliament following his party’s election defeat marks a seismic shift in Hungarian politics. This theme, ongoing from the previous run, reflects Orbán’s efforts to reorganize Fidesz and maintain influence amid rising opposition from Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party. The story resonates across Europe as a potential turning point for right-wing populism.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán’s resignation from parliament and focus on reorganizing Fidesz dominate coverage, with debates over his successor and the party’s future direction.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party gains momentum, with Magyar announcing his cabinet and negotiating EU funds, signaling a potential shift in Hungary’s political landscape.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU leaders react to Orbán’s resignation and the Tisza Party’s rise, with discussions over Hungary’s access to frozen EU funds and compliance with rule-of-law conditions.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Incidents of political violence and vandalism targeting far-right parties and leaders have surged across Europe, reflecting a broader trend of escalating tensions. This theme, ongoing from the previous run, highlights how far-right rhetoric—particularly around immigration and national identity—is provoking backlash from left-wing and anarchist groups, with coverage emphasizing the risks of polarization and extremism.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega offices vandalized with anti-Salvini graffiti during Liberation Day protests, with coverage framing the incidents as part of a broader pattern of political violence.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox rallies in Cádiz and other regions face protests and counter-demonstrations, with coverage highlighting clashes between far-right supporters and left-wing groups.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK candidates face scrutiny over Islamophobic and extremist social media posts, with coverage linking the party’s rhetoric to broader concerns about political violence.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN faces protests and legal challenges, with coverage emphasizing the party’s role in fueling political tensions and extremism.
